name: Button

notes: Figma name is 'Inverse-button' — designed for use on dark/inverted backgrounds.
The 'State=focus' variant uses the pressed-strength state layer, not the hover-strength one. Focus receives a stronger visual affordance than hover.
Pressed and Disabled states have no Figma variants. Both are required for a complete implementation.
Icon Button (nodeId: 160:264) is a related but separate component — icon-only, circular, no label.

description: A low-emphasis ghost button used for the lowest-priority actions. The container is invisible at rest and becomes visible only on interaction. Designed for use on dark or inverted backgrounds. Supports a dynamic text label and the standard click interaction.

label.description: The visible text inside the button. Always rendered; this component has no icon-only mode.

disabled.description: When true, suppresses all user interaction and removes the button from the tab order. The button still renders its label.

type.description: Native HTML button type. 'submit' triggers the closest ancestor form on activation. 'reset' resets the closest ancestor form. 'button' does neither.

onClick.description: Callback invoked when the user activates the button via click, Space, or Enter. Not called when disabled.

aria-label.description: Overrides the label text as the accessible name. Use when the visible label alone is insufficient for screen reader context.

aria-pressed.description: Expresses a toggle state when the button controls a persistent on/off condition (e.g., a bold toggle in a text editor). Omit if the button is not a toggle.

aria-expanded.description: Set to true or false when the button controls the visibility of another element (e.g., a dropdown). Omit otherwise.

aria-controls.description: ID of the element this button controls. Required when aria-expanded is used.

rest.description: Default idle state. Container is invisible; only the label is visible.

hovered.description: Pointer is over the button. Container becomes visible with a subtle state layer overlay.
entry_conditions: pointerenter fires and disabled is false

focused.description: Button has keyboard focus. Container becomes visible with a stronger state layer overlay than hover. A visible focus indicator is also required.
entry_conditions: tab or shift+tab navigates to this button
pointer click that also gives focus (browser-dependent)
exit_conditions: tab or shift+tab moves focus away

pressed.description: Button is being held down (pointer down or keyboard key held). Momentary active state before release triggers the action.
entry_conditions: pointerdown fires while hovered and not disabled
Space or Enter held down while focused and not disabled
exit_conditions: Space or Enter is released (activates the button)

disabled.description: All interaction is suppressed. Label remains visible. The button is removed from the tab order and cannot be activated.

transitions.guard: pointer is still over the button
pointer leaves while button is held

transitions.note: Activation fires on key release, then state returns to focused

activate_action.description: The primary action. Fires when the user releases a click or keyboard key on the button.
effects: if type is 'submit', submits the closest ancestor <form>
if type is 'reset', resets the closest ancestor <form>

enter.effect: transitions to hovered if not disabled

leave.effect: transitions to rest; cancels any in-progress press

down.description: Pointer button pressed over the button.
down.effect: transitions to pressed if not disabled

up.effect: activates the button if released over it; transitions back to hovered or rest

tab.effect: moves focus to this button if it is next in tab order and not disabled

shift_tab.effect: moves focus to this button if it is previous in tab order and not disabled

space.description: Activates the button while focused. Fires on key-up.
space.effect: invokes activate_action on release if not disabled

enter.description: Activates the button while focused. Fires on key-up.
enter.effect: invokes activate_action on release if not disabled; also submits the ancestor form if type='submit'

escape.description: No inherent behavior on a plain button. Parent patterns (dialogs, menus) may intercept this.

tap.effect: equivalent to pointer.click — invokes activate_action if not disabled

long_press.description: No inherent behavior for a plain button.
long_press.effect: none unless parent context defines a long-press action

resize.description: The button's container width expands to fit its label text plus padding. No behavioral change occurs at any container breakpoint — the button does not change modes, collapse, or hide based on available space.

disabled_prop_change.description: Parent sets disabled to true or false.
disabled_prop_change.effect: transitions to disabled state or back to rest; removes from or restores tab order

label_prop_change.effect: label text updates immediately; no state transition; no event emitted

focus_call.description: Imperative focus() on the button's DOM node.
focus_call.effect: transitions to focused state if not disabled

blur_call.description: Imperative blur() on the button's DOM node.

click_call.description: Imperative .click() on the button's DOM node.
click_call.effect: invokes activate_action; behaves identically to a user click including form submission

activate.description: The button's sole user-facing action — executes the associated operation.
effects: if type='submit': submits closest ancestor <form> and triggers form's submit event
if type='reset': resets closest ancestor <form> fields to their default values
does_not: toggle unless onClick manages a toggle externally

click.description: Fires when the user successfully activates the button. This is the native DOM click event — consumers listen via onClick prop.

payload.event: MouseEvent or KeyboardEvent — the originating browser event

not_emitted_when: pointer.down fires but pointer.up fires outside the button

accessibility.element: Use a native <button> element. Do not use <div role='button'> — native buttons receive keyboard focus, form submission, and Space/Enter activation for free.

aria_attributes.aria-label: overrides the label text as the accessible name when provided; use when visible label is ambiguous in context
aria_attributes.aria-disabled: set to 'true' when disabled; prefer the native disabled attribute on <button> for automatic tab order removal
aria_attributes.aria-pressed: present only when the button controls a binary on/off state; value reflects current toggle state (true | false)
aria_attributes.aria-expanded: present only when the button controls the visibility of another region; value reflects whether that region is open (true | false)
aria_attributes.aria-controls: required alongside aria-expanded; references the ID of the controlled region

keyboard_navigation.tab_stop: included in tab order when not disabled; removed when disabled
keyboard_navigation.enter: activates on key-up; also submits the ancestor form if type='submit'
keyboard_navigation.escape: no built-in behavior; parent containers intercept if needed

focus_management.focus_visible: a visible focus ring must appear when focus arrives via keyboard; must not appear on pointer click in browsers that support :focus-visible
focus_management.focus_on_disable: if the button holds focus when disabled, the browser moves focus to the next focusable element; do not call blur() explicitly
focus_management.focus_after_action: focus remains on the button after activation unless onClick explicitly moves it (e.g., opening a dialog that traps focus)

screen_reader.on_activate: the accessible name (label or aria-label) is announced; the role 'button' is announced by the reader
screen_reader.on_disable: if focused when disabled, 'dimmed' or 'unavailable' is announced depending on the reader
screen_reader.on_aria_expanded_change: the new expanded state ('collapsed' or 'expanded') is announced

responsive.description: The button sizes to its content (label + padding). It does not change behavioral mode at any viewport or container size. No collapse, truncation, or mode-switch behavior is defined.

parent_requirements.description: The Button is a leaf component. It places no structural requirements on its parent.
parent_requirements.layout: none — parent can place it anywhere; inline, flex, grid all work
parent_requirements.context: none — does not consume any context or provider
parent_requirements.form_association: if type='submit' or type='reset', the button must be a descendant of a <form> element or use the 'form' HTML attribute to associate with a form by ID
parent_requirements.label_association: the label prop provides the accessible name directly; no external label element is required unless aria-labelledby is used instead

mutual_exclusions.rule: hovered and disabled are mutually exclusive
mutual_exclusions.reason: a disabled button cannot be interacted with; pointer presence must not produce the hovered visual or trigger any action

mutual_exclusions.rule: aria-pressed and type='submit' should not coexist
mutual_exclusions.reason: a submit button performs a one-shot action; toggle semantics (aria-pressed) apply to persistent binary states and conflict with submit behavior

conditional_behaviors.then: all pointer, keyboard, and touch triggers are suppressed; onClick is never called; form submission does not occur
pressing Enter while the button is focused also submits the form (in addition to Space); the form's own validation and submit handler fire first
activation resets all form fields to their defaultValue; this cannot be prevented by onClick returning false — use a custom handler to intercept
aria-controls must also be provided with a valid element ID; the button is responsible for updating aria-expanded when the controlled region opens or closes

required_combinations.reason: screen readers use aria-controls to announce which element is being expanded; without it, the expanded state is announced with no associated target

scenarios.user_clicks: Button is at rest. User moves pointer over it — container becomes visible (hovered). User presses and holds — pressed state activates. User releases — activate_action fires, onClick is called, click event emits. State returns to hovered.
scenarios.keyboard_activation: User tabs to the button. Focused state activates with stronger container visibility and a focus ring. User presses Space. Pressed state activates momentarily. User releases Space. activate_action fires, onClick is called. State returns to focused.
scenarios.form_submission: Button has type='submit'. User presses Enter while focused. Form validation runs. If valid, form submit event fires. onClick is also called. Button remains focused.
scenarios.disabled_attempt: Button has disabled=true. User hovers, clicks, or presses Space. No state change. No visual response. onClick is not called. Tab skips over the button.
scenarios.toggle_button: Button has aria-pressed=false. User clicks. onClick fires. Parent updates aria-pressed to true. Screen reader announces 'pressed'. User clicks again. onClick fires. Parent updates aria-pressed to false. Screen reader announces 'not pressed'.
scenarios.controls_dropdown: Button has aria-expanded=false and aria-controls='menu-id'. User clicks. onClick fires. Parent opens the dropdown and sets aria-expanded=true. Screen reader announces 'expanded'. User presses Escape inside the dropdown. Parent closes dropdown, sets aria-expanded=false, and returns focus to this button.
